steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice "emphasizes a difference" between the two species' eggs. To emphasize a difference, the sentence must do two things: state both measurements and use explicit contrast language that flags one as different from the other. Simply mentioning both species or both numbers without contrast language does not emphasize a difference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'A 2017 study compared the lengths of eggs...': mentions both species as examples of "an array of different bird species" but gives neither measurement. No numbers, no contrast — it describes a study's scope, not a difference. ✗
  • Choice B — 'The bird species Acrocephalus scirpaceus...and Alcedo atthis...were included in a 2017 study...': names both species and notes their taxonomic orders but gives no egg-length numbers and no contrast language. The difference between the eggs is not stated at all. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Mary Caswell Stoddard led a research study that determined the average lengths...including those of Acrocephalus scirpaceus birds (1.80 cm) and Alcedo atthis birds (2.19 cm).': provides both measurements, but lists them with "and" — presenting them as parallel items, not as a contrast. A reader must do the math themselves; the difference is not emphasized. ✗
  • Choice D — 'A 2017 study found that Acrocephalus scirpaceus eggs had an average length of 1.80 cm, whereas Alcedo atthis eggs were longer, with an average length of 2.19 cm.': gives both measurements and uses "whereas" plus the explicit word "longer" to directly flag the difference. This is the only choice that both quantifies and emphasizes the contrast. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
